_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-20R Diazepam (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-20R: Diazepam — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
contraindications: Pediatric use for procedural sedation — NOT indicated (use midazolam instead)
Known hypersensitivity to diazepam or benzodiazepines
precautions: ADULT ONLY for procedural sedation in Michigan EMS protocol — NOT for pediatric procedural sedation
Titrate slowly — respiratory depression and apnea risk
Have airway management equipment (BVM, advanced airway supplies) immediately available
Monitor respiratory rate, SpO2, and mental status continuously during and after administration
Flumazenil (benzodiazepine reversal) is not a Michigan EMS protocol medication — manage respiratory depression with airway support
notes: Diazepam is for procedural sedation in ADULTS ONLY in Michigan EMS protocol
For pediatric procedural sedation: use midazolam (9-34R)
Dose: 5-10 mg (0.1 mg/kg) titrated slowly IV; may repeat q5 minutes; maximum 0.3 mg/kg total
If opioid also administered for pain: reduce benzodiazepine dose and monitor respiratory status very closely

dosing.dose: 5-10 mg (0.1 mg/kg) titrated slowly; repeat q5 minutes to effect; maximum 0.3 mg/kg total
dosing.notes: Titrate to effect; have airway management ready; NOT for pediatric procedural sedation
